content: By 2025, the self-improving memory architecture of curiosity-driven autonomous self-supervised learning agents can be reframed as a recursive nested agent system, where each episodic memory chunk acts as a tiny, specialized sub-agent that proactively contributes to the main agent’s learning loop rather than passively storing data. This reengineering of memory turns static storage into a distributed team of curious sub-agents that actively surface relevant gaps for the main agent to explore.
